title: OPUS 4.8!!! (also maybe GPT5.6??)
summary: Matthew Berman discusses Anthropic's Opus 4.8 release and speculates about a potential GPT 5.6 from OpenAI. The video appears to cover updates to these frontier language models.
writeup: A livestream walkthrough of the Claude Opus 4.8 release, covering the model's headline claims, benchmark suite, and new Claude Code features. The through-line is a tension between official benchmarks and real-world perception: Opus 4.8 posts a 5-point SWE-bench Pro jump to 69.2% over Opus 4.7 (released just 6 weeks prior), yet the newer DeepSuite benchmark tells a different story, showing GPT 5.5 at 70% versus Opus 4.7 at 54%, which better matches developer vibe-checks. Pricing holds at Opus 4.7 levels while a fast mode delivers roughly 2.5x throughput.
key_points: Claude Opus 4.8 ships at the same price as Opus 4.7, making the capability improvement an effective cost reduction per unit of work, and arrives only 6 weeks after 4.7, illustrating the accelerating cadence of frontier releases.

SWE-bench Pro shows Opus 4.8 at 69.2%, a 5-point gain over Opus 4.7, but DeepSuite (presented as more reflective of real-world coding feel) puts GPT 5.5 at 70% versus Opus 4.7 at 54%, raising serious doubts about SWE-bench Pro as a discriminating signal at high score levels.
Opus 4.8 fast mode runs at approximately 2.5x the token generation speed of the standard mode, enabled via /fast in Claude Code or through the API, with broader access gated behind a waitlist.

On Humanity's Last Exam (multidisciplinary reasoning), Opus 4.8 leads the field by a wide margin, roughly 3 points above Opus 4.7 and substantially above GPT 5.5 (41.4% without tools, 52.2% with tools); on agentic terminal coding (TerminalBench 2.1), GPT 5.5 holds a clear lead at 78.2%.

Dynamic workflows, a research preview in Claude Code, lets Opus 4.8 plan a task, spin up hundreds of parallel subagents to execute it, and verify the result before reporting back, with large-scale file migrations cited as the target use case.

Financial analysis is emerging as a deliberate second vertical for Anthropic alongside coding: Finance Agent v2 results show a 2-point improvement for Opus 4.8, and the presenter notes the significant market opportunity if the model can reliably handle financial knowledge work.

chapters.title: Fast Mode and Claude Code Improvements
